description: PostHog Analytics integration with feature flag, dynamic script loading, type-safe events, and provider component
steps: Add analytics_enabled feature flag to prisma/feature-flags.ts with config: { provider: 'posthog', projectApiKey: '', host: 'https://app.posthog.com' }

Create lib/analytics/events.ts - Type-safe event name constants and payload types: product_viewed, add_to_cart, remove_from_cart, checkout_started, order_placed, search_performed, offer_viewed, promo_code_applied

Create lib/analytics/posthog.ts - PostHog client wrapper that dynamically loads posthog-js via script tag when initialized. Exports init(apiKey, host), capture(event, properties), identify(userId, traits), reset(). All methods are no-ops if not initialized.

Create lib/analytics/index.ts - Analytics abstraction layer that checks analytics_enabled flag (client-side: checks NEXT_PUBLIC_POSTHOG_KEY env var presence). Exports trackEvent(name, props), identifyUser(id, traits). Delegates to posthog.ts.

Create components/analytics-provider.tsx - Client component that reads NEXT_PUBLIC_POSTHOG_KEY and NEXT_PUBLIC_POSTHOG_HOST env vars. If both present, initializes PostHog via lib/analytics/posthog.ts on mount. Renders children only. Handles page_view tracking automatically via Next.js router events.
Add AnalyticsProvider to components/providers.tsx - Import and render AnalyticsProvider wrapping children (only renders script if env vars are set, graceful no-op otherwise)
Add test in tests/analytics-events.test.ts verifying event type definitions and that capture function handles missing initialization gracefully
acceptance_criteria: Feature flag analytics_enabled exists in prisma/feature-flags.ts with enabled: false
lib/analytics/posthog.ts loads PostHog via dynamic script tag, no npm package
components/analytics-provider.tsx only initializes when NEXT_PUBLIC_POSTHOG_KEY is present
All analytics functions are no-ops when not initialized (no errors thrown)
Event types are defined for all 8+ events listed in requirements
verification: Run npx eslint . from apps/web/ and confirm no errors
Run npm run test from apps/web/ and confirm analytics tests pass
